danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile character sprites (zombies, skeletons, bats, rats, spiders, minotaurs, cats, dogs)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring combat or avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Decreasing meter UI element; automatic drain when stationary
danger_signals.meaning: Approaching vulnerability state; character falling asleep
Shift to alternate dangerous state where player is more vulnerable to Freddy Krueger attacks

danger_signals.visual_form: All bones collected within building area
Inaccessible building or pathway during midnight setting
danger_signals.meaning: Danger or restriction; requires key item to progress

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible coffee cup sprite scattered throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Restores Sleep Meter; prevents/delays transition to Dream World

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible boombox sprite in Dream World areas
safety_signals.meaning: Returns player character to default (safer) version of level from Dream World

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible key item after defeating Freddy in an area
safety_signals.meaning: Unlocks new safe passage to unexplored areas; progression reward

safety_signals.visual_form: Special collectible icons appearing in Dream World
safety_signals.meaning: Grants temporary enhanced abilities (shuriken, javelin, magic projectiles) for survival

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bones scattered by Freddy Krueger (collection objective); presence of coffee and boombox items as survival resources

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Freddy Krueger boss entity; Dream World manifestation (more aggressive state)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dream World environment itself; hostile creature spawns (zombies, skeletons, bats, rats, spiders, minotaurs); locked areas requiring keys

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Non-humanoid enemies (cats, dogs, bats, rats, spiders); skeleton and zombie variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Elm Street vicinity setting; high school furnace (final objective location); buildings with locked access

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard environment rendering; stable screen presentation; accessible areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Slower Sleep Meter drain when moving; normal enemy behavior; ability to collect bones and progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sleep Meter UI visibly depleted; visual warning state building
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player character vulnerability increasing; enemies remain standard threat level

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Dream World (Any player Sleep Meter fully depleted)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Movielike wavering/distortion filter applied to entire screen; alternate visual rendering of same environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All players transported to Dream World regardless of individual meter state; Freddy Krueger becomes significantly more dangerous and aggressive; increased attack vulnerability; special power-ups become available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental change signaling completion; visual readiness indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss battle with Freddy Krueger triggers; upon victory, new key becomes available; new area becomes accessible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark environment palette; nighttime aesthetic; certain buildings appear locked or obscured
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Restricted area access; enemy spawns; hostile supernatural presence pervasive

environmental_storytelling.example: Freddy Krueger's bones scattered throughout Elm Street vicinity communicate his defeat/dismemberment and the core objective of recollection and destruction
High school furnace as final destination for bone disposal creates narrative closure tied to film franchise (Freddy's death method); spatial goal reinforces story
Locked buildings and key-gated progression imply supernatural sealing or danger containment; midnight setting creates horror atmosphere through access denial
Screen wavering effect communicates psychological horror; visual distortion serves as both gameplay mechanic and narrative device showing character consciousness state
Mix of supernatural (zombies, skeletons, bats, spiders) and mundane hostile creatures (cats, dogs, rats) suggests corruption of ordinary environment by Freddy's supernatural influence

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can move freely; collects bones and items; engaged in standard platforming combat
Remains functionally capable but increasingly vulnerable; visual/audio warnings implied
Significantly increased vulnerability to attacks; access to special power-ups (projectile weapons); behavior shift toward survival-focused gameplay

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Hostile creatures (zombies, skeletons, bats, rats, spiders, minotaurs)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Aggressive attack patterns; attempt to drain player life/health; spawned throughout levels as persistent threats
Challenging boss battle; appears after bone collection; defeat grants area exit and key
Dramatically increased aggression; heightened danger; 'bizarrely weak' characterization noted by critics suggests design disconnect from film threat level
Stationary; collectible; immediately restore Sleep Meter upon contact
Stationary; collectible; immediately returns player to normal world state upon contact

notes: This game employs a unique 'Sleep Meter' mechanic as primary world-state indicator, creating dual-environment horror dynamic (normal Elm Street vs. Dream World). Danger communication relies heavily on UI meter depletion and visual screen distortion rather than environmental color coding. Faction distinction is implicit rather than explicit—teenagers vs. Supernatural threat without clear visual faction identifiers (no colored armor, insignia, or faction-specific environmental markers). The game's manifestation layer depends on item collection (coffee/boombox) as safety signals and screen wavering as the primary world-state visual communication. Environmental storytelling primarily through location design (furnace objective) and enemy ecosystem (supernatural creatures infesting ordinary neighborhood). Critical reception noted the screen wavering effect as effective horror presentation despite overall game weakness.
